relatedness_reasoning: The inbound file contains loan applications with applicant personal data and a required compliance check type 'Sanctions Screening'; the outbound file is a sanctions list hit notification. Risk and Compliance receives applications, performs sanctions screening, and sends results back to Credit. The applicant data (name, DOB) maps to matched entity data, and the sanctions screening check type directly motivates the outbound message.

mappings.reasoning: System-generated unique identifier for the notification message; no source in inbound.
Message generation timestamp; not directly sourced from inbound application data.
Outbound source is 'risk_and_compliance', which is the destination_department in inbound metadata.
Outbound destination is 'credit', which is the source_department in inbound metadata.
Unique identifier for the sanctions match event; generated by screening system, not in inbound.
Risk severity assigned by sanctions screening engine; not derivable from inbound application data.
Workflow status of the sanctions hit review; generated by compliance system, not in inbound.
Customer ID from inbound becomes the internal entity identifier for the matched entity.
Entity type is fixed as 'INDIVIDUAL' for loan applicants; not explicitly stated in inbound schema.
Applicant full_name is used as the matched entity name for sanctions screening.
Applicant date_of_birth is passed to matched entity dateOfBirth for screening comparison.
Nationality is not present in inbound applicant data; would require external enrichment.
Identification documents are not included in inbound loan application schema.
Sanctions lists matched are determined by the screening engine; not in inbound data.
Risk factors are identified during screening; not derivable from inbound application.
Associated bank accounts are not present in inbound loan application data.
Review assignment and workflow details are generated by compliance system post-screening.
Audit trail is generated by the screening engine; not sourced from inbound application.
